Title: git remote set-url origin https://github.com/rhage-CS/Game1-b-Veldmoor-
Credits: A detective story in a walled kingdom city. Find the truth.

DetectiveOffice.Body: Your office. Three case files sit on the desk — a dead merchant, a missing noble heir, a dockside informant who went quiet. All came in the same week. Where do you start?

MarketSquare.Body: Market square. Stalls packed tight. The spot where the merchant fell is roped off at the far end.

Choices.Text: Talk to the barterwoman at the spice stall

SpiceStall.Body: Sela the barterwoman glances around before speaking. 'The merchant had enemies. A man with a signet ring came by the week before he died. I'd seen that ring before — on a noble.' She won't say more.

BurnedStall.Body: The stall is ash and scorched wood. Among the debris you find a small soot-caked vial. Something was stored here that someone wanted gone.

Choices.Text: Take the ash fragment and leave

Apothecary.Body: Cluttered shelves. The apothecary is nervous. One jar has a false bottom — inside is a signet ring with a crest you don't recognise. Yet.

Choices.Text: Take the signet ring and leave

BackAlley.Body: Torr, a beggar who sees everything, leans against the wall. 'That merchant dealt in things that weren't his. Someone from the docks arranged it. Same someone who keeps the old warehouse locked.'

NobleGate.Body: The entrance to the Noble Quarter. Guards wave you through.

LordsManor.Body: A large stone manor. The butler says the Lord is unavailable.

Choices.Text: Talk to the cook in the kitchen

ManorKitchen.Body: The cook Berta talks without looking up. 'The Lord had a visitor three nights before the merchant died. Came in through the back. I saw the ring — same one the merchant wore sometimes.'

LordsStudy.Body: Books everywhere. On the desk, beneath a ledger, is a wax seal — the same crest as the signet ring from the apothecary.

Choices.Text: Take the wax seal and leave

Cathedral.Body: High ceilings, cold stone. The archives are kept by monk Aldus. There is a narrow door behind the altar with a wax-seal slot.

Choices.Text: Talk to monk Aldus in the vestry
Try the door behind the altar
Choices.LockedText: The door has a wax-seal slot. You don't have anything to fit it.

Vestry.Body: Aldus pulls a record without being asked. 'The heir was moved three months ago. Not dead — hidden. The seal on the transfer document matches a private noble house, not the crown.'

HiddenPassage.Body: A narrow corridor behind the altar. At the end: a small room with a child's cot and a name carved into the wall. The heir was here. Recently.

MainPier.Body: The docks. Salt air, crates stacked high. The harbormaster's post is ahead, the old lighthouse to the left.

Choices.LockedText: The warehouse door has a wax-seal slot. You don't have the right signet to open it.

HarbormasterPost.Body: A heavy shipping ledger sits chained to the desk. The harbormaster is out.

OldLighthouse.Body: The lighthouse hasn't been lit in years. Under a loose board: a rolled cargo manifest — shipments that never appeared in any official record. Destination: the Noble Quarter.

Choices.Text: Take the cargo manifest and leave

FishMarket.Body: Dag the fishmonger guts a fish without looking up. 'I saw them unloading crates at midnight. No markings. One man wore a signet ring. Same ring I've seen on that Lord up the hill.'

Warehouse.Body: The signet ring fits the slot. The door swings open. Inside: unregistered goods and a copy of the shipping ledger with a name written clearly on the inside cover — the royal advisor.

Choices.Text: Take the ledger copy and leave

SlumCrossroads.Body: The lower city. Narrow streets. The Cracked Flagon tavern is to the left. The guard barracks ahead. A pawnshop in the corner.

CrackedFlagon.Body: Smoky and loud. The barmaid leans over the counter. 'The guard captain takes payments from someone up the hill. Comes in flush every week, orders the good stuff.'

GuardBarracks.Body: Mostly empty. A junior guard won't meet your eyes when you mention the captain.

CaptainsOffice.Body: The captain is calm when he sees you. Too calm. 'I run a clean post.' There's a forged coin on his desk — the kind used to pay off city informants. He doesn't explain it.

Pawnshop.Body: Among the tray of items: a noble's passage token — gets you through the High City gate — and a forged coin.

Choices.Text: Take the noble's pass and forged coin

SewerEntrance.Body: A hatch behind the barracks. Padlocked — but the lock takes a forged coin in place of a key. City corruption runs deep.

Choices.Text: Use the forged coin and go down
Choices.LockedText: The hatch is padlocked. You need something to work it open.

SewerTunnels.Body: Damp stone. Scratched into the wall: a cipher and a partial name. The same crest as the wax seal. Someone used these tunnels regularly and left a trail.

Choices.Text: Note the cipher and head back up

HighCityGate.Body: The gate to the High City. The guard looks you over.

Choices.Text: Show the noble's pass and enter
Choices.LockedText: The guard stops you. 'No passage without a token.'

CrescentPromenade.Body: A wide boulevard. Quieter than the rest of the city. Old money lives up here.

AuctionFloor.Body: Nobles bidding on objects. Master Erren runs the podium with practiced calm.

Choices.LockedText: The back room is bolted. You need the auction ledger as proof of inquiry before Erren will open it.

AuctioneerPodium.Body: Erren lowers his voice. 'A signet ring matching that crest came through here six months ago. Private buyer, unmarked coin. I kept a record — it's in the back room, but I need the auction ledger as proof before I open it to anyone.'

BackRoom.Body: The buyer's name is listed. It matches the warehouse ledger copy exactly. The royal advisor purchased the ring the same week the merchant was killed.

ReadingRoom.Body: Scholar Veyra is surrounded by open books. She looks up when you mention the cipher.

Choices.LockedText: Veyra asks if you have the cipher. You don't have it yet.

Observatory.Body: Veyra leads you to the rooftop. She traces the cipher's origin — it was used in official palace correspondence. Only the royal advisor had access. She hands you a decryption key she made from the pattern.

ClockShopFront.Body: Clocks everywhere. One on the wall is stopped at the wrong hour — the hour the merchant was killed.

Choices.Text: Talk to Clockmaker Fen in the workshop

ClockWorkshop.Body: Fen is evasive but nervous, glancing at a large cabinet clock in the corner. You examine it and find a hidden compartment inside — the auction ledger, taken for safekeeping by someone who knew what it proved.

Choices.Text: Take the auction ledger and leave

BathHall.Body: Steam and low conversation. Attendant Mira is folding towels in the corner.

AttendantRoom.Body: Mira is nervous. 'A lady came in distressed last month. Said she'd seen something she wasn't meant to. Her brooch went missing that same day — she thought it was taken to keep her quiet.'

ChangingRoom.Body: In the back of a locker, wrapped in cloth: a brooch with a noble crest. Taken and hidden here. The crest matches the manor.

Choices.Text: Take the stolen brooch and leave

RuinsExterior.Body: A manor, recently collapsed. The stonework is scorched. This didn't fall on its own.

Shelter.Body: Behind a fallen wall: Pia, a young servant. She's frightened but talks. 'I was there. I saw who ordered it. The royal advisor came himself. Said the manor had to disappear along with everyone in it.' She writes down what she saw and hands it to you.

PalaceGates.Body: The palace gates. Two guards stand watch. They won't let you through without hard evidence.

Choices.LockedText: The guards won't move. You need a ledger copy and a witness account before they'll let you through.
The guards won't move. You need a ledger copy and a witness account before they'll let you through.

PalaceCourtyard.Body: You're inside. A royal blacksmith works near the armory. The advisor's chambers are to the right.

RoyalArmory.Body: The blacksmith recognises the crest on the wax seal immediately. 'That's the advisor's private seal. He had it made here six months ago. Paid extra to keep it off the record.' He hands you a palace access key.

Choices.Text: Take the palace key and leave

Choices.LockedText: The door is locked. You need a key.

InnerSanctum.Body: The advisor's private room. Among the documents: orders to move the heir, payments to the guard captain, and a letter arranging the merchant's death. Everything signed.

Choices.Text: Take the documents and go to the throne room

ThroneRoom.Body: You lay the evidence before the king — the ledger copy, Pia's testimony, the signed orders, the auction record, the cipher. The advisor is brought in. He says nothing. The king says enough. The heir is found within the day. The merchant's family is told the truth. The guard captain is arrested by nightfall. You walk out into cool air and go back to your office. The desk is clear.
